why_this_warning: This label is based on validation accuracy, geography coverage, and whether forecasts hit the model's lower or upper bound. When many forecasts hit a bound, maps and rankings can become less informative because place-to-place differences are compressed.

summary.warning_text: Primary State benchmark. The statistical baseline is transparent, stable, and performs well out of sample.
summary.reason_for_label: coverage 100.0%; reference-bound share 0.3%; peak year bound share 2.0%; differentiated views 100.0%; validation RMSE 1.74; test RMSE 1.76; test R^2 0.894; selected as the default benchmark for this geography

summary.warning_text: State robustness benchmark. Tree ML has strong held-out accuracy and is shown as a robustness benchmark rather than the primary State benchmark.
summary.reason_for_label: coverage 100.0%; reference-bound share 0.0%; peak year bound share 0.0%; differentiated views 100.0%; validation RMSE 1.47; test RMSE 1.25; test R^2 0.947

summary.warning_text: Benchmark only. Useful as a nonlinear robustness check, but not the primary state specification.
summary.reason_for_label: coverage 100.0%; reference-bound share 0.0%; peak year bound share 0.0%; differentiated views 100.0%; validation RMSE 1.52; test RMSE 1.26; test R^2 0.946; kept as a robustness benchmark rather than the primary interpretation

summary.warning_text: Use caution for MSA maps. Many MSA forecasts hit the model's lower bound, so maps and rankings may understate differences across places. Some scenario changes also flatten to zero when both paths land on the same bound. Held-out test fit is also weak.
summary.reason_for_label: coverage 100.0%; reference-bound share 83.7%; peak year bound share 100.0%; differentiated views 57.1%; validation RMSE 18.05; test RMSE 19.82; test R^2 -0.300

summary.warning_text: Preferred MSA display model. This model preserves metropolitan variation and avoids lower-bound forecast problems. However, MSA forecasts are uncertain and should be interpreted as exploratory spatial patterns, not precise predictions.
summary.reason_for_label: coverage 100.0%; reference-bound share 0.0%; peak year bound share 0.0%; differentiated views 75.0%; validation RMSE 17.33; test RMSE 16.83; test R^2 0.063; selected as the default benchmark for this geography

summary.warning_text: Use caution for MSA maps. Many MSA forecasts hit the model's lower bound, so maps and rankings may understate differences across places. Some scenario changes also flatten to zero when both paths land on the same bound.

scenario_diagnostics.availability_reason: Available for MSAs using parent-state scenario proxy inputs merged onto each MSA.
Supported with ACS and ATUS MSA inputs.

Supported with ACS and ATUS MSA inputs.

geo_notes.state: The State view is the primary forecast view. State models are based on fewer, larger, more stable geographies and have stronger held-out performance. The statistical baseline is the primary State benchmark because it is transparent, stable, and performs well out of sample. Tree ML has strong held-out accuracy and is shown as a robustness benchmark.
geo_notes.msa: The Metro patterns view is exploratory. It helps users see whether digital-life scenarios may matter more in some metropolitan labor markets than others, but it is best used for broad spatial patterns rather than exact local forecasts. Tree ML is preferred for MSA display because it preserves metropolitan variation and avoids lower-bound forecast problems, not because the Metro view is intended for exact local forecasting.
